_comment: Single source of truth for ELO tier bands. Read by the backend (ranking_service.tier_bands_for + apply_tiers) and by the web frontend (positional-tiers.html, via GET /api/tier-config); mobile caches the same JSON at boot (utils/tierBands.ts). Every (scoring_format, position, tier) row carries a [min, max] ELO band. The backend's apply_tiers spreads ELOs linearly inside [min, max]; bucketing walks tiers top-down and assigns the first tier whose min <= elo (tier_for_elo).
_calibration: Recalibrated 2026-07-11 (pick-value tier ladder). Tiers now read directly in draft-pick terms: each tier's floor is a rung of the anchor/pick Elo ladder (GENERIC_PICK_SEEDS in backend/server.py + the multi-first anchor Elos). firsts_2plus floor = 1788, just under value_to_elo(2 x Mid 1st) = 1788.6 (all 2/3/4-firsts anchor pins land here); first_1 floor = Late 1st seed 1580 (worth a 1st-round pick); second floor = Late 2nd 1400; third floor = Late 3rd 1280; fourth floor = Late 4th 1220; bench = below 4th-round value down to 1150 (below 1150 = unranked, which keeps the no_value anchor at 1100 below every band). Because pick value is position-uniform by design (see docs/cross-client-invariants.md, Pick anchor keys), the bands are identical across positions AND scoring formats — the per-(format, position) shape is kept so consumers do not change; occupancy differs per position/format because the seed Elos do. Occupancy is pinned by backend/tests/test_tier_occupancy.py against a checked-in DP snapshot — re-run it after editing these numbers.
